architecture.summary: FlowEdit augments a frozen flow-matching TTS model with a Modern Hopfield Network serving as content-addressable episodic memory for pronunciation corrections. When corrective feedback is provided, the system optimizes a token-level perturbation in the text embedding space of the frozen model's input. At inference, a learned retrieval mechanism with soft attention and a similarity gate queries the Hopfield memory to retrieve stored corrections, enabling fuzzy morphological matching across out-of-vocabulary proper nouns without retraining the base model.
trained_components: soft attention retrieval mechanism with similarity gate

biblio.title: FlowEdit: Associative Memory for Lifelong Pronunciation Adaptation in Flow-Matching TTS

claims.dataset: curated benchmark of 312 multilingual proper nouns across 18 language families
claims.statement: FlowEdit reduces target-word Phoneme Error Rate by 92.7% relative to the zero-shot baseline
FlowEdit maintains identical general-speech quality while enabling pronunciation corrections
Corrections complete in approximately 15 seconds on a single GPU
The system enables fuzzy morphological matching through soft attention with a similarity gate
FlowEdit learns pronunciation corrections as latent conditioning edits rather than weight updates to support lifelong adaptation in frozen flow-matching TTS
